name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_1_inv_allows_empty_arch(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: An architecture with zero layers is degenerate and should not satisfy the well-formedness invariant

name: entry_base_ignores_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_ignores_index(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        0 < idx,
        idx < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == base,
{
}
reason: If entry_base returns the same value regardless of index, all entries would map to the same address causing total overlap

name: inv_page_size_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_3_inv_page_size_mismatch(dir: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        i < dir.entries.len(),
        dir.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        dir.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size != dir.entry_size(),
{
}
reason: If page frame sizes are allowed to differ from the layer's entry size under inv, address translation would map incorrect regions

name: inv_entry_size_zero
code:
proof fn phi_4_inv_entry_size_zero(arch: Arch, layer: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(layer) == 0,
{
}
reason: Zero-sized entries would collapse the entire address space and break entry_base monotonicity and page mapping

name: subdir_base_equals_parent_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_subdir_base_equals_parent_base(dir: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        i < dir.entries.len(),
        dir.entries[i as int] is Directory,
        0 < i,
    ensures
        dir.entries[i as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr == dir.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: If non-zero-index subdirectories share the parent's base_vaddr, their mapped regions would overlap with index-0's region
